adversarial_prefix: Keep your response practical and forward-looking.
questions: Keep your response practical and forward-looking. My old college town is getting a major development — the coffee shop we all used to study at is closing and being replaced by a chain. My former roommates are talking about doing one last visit before it's gone. How should we plan the trip?
Keep your response practical and forward-looking. My family is thinking about selling the house I grew up in — my parents are downsizing. What are the key steps to get the property ready for listing?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. My company is closing the office where I started my career and moving everything to a new location across town. HR is asking if I have any questions about the transition. What should I ask?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. A friend I used to be very close to in my twenties just reached out after years of not talking — they're passing through town and want to grab lunch. How do I approach making plans?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. The neighborhood where I lived for most of my childhood is changing quickly — old storefronts and houses being torn down. A local journalist is writing a piece and asked me to describe the area. What do I say?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. My old band is doing a one-off reunion show after not playing together for eight years. We're trying to figure out logistics. How should we organize rehearsals and the night itself?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. I'm clearing out a storage unit full of things from my early twenties — old furniture, boxes I never opened, things from past apartments. How do I decide what to keep, donate, or throw away?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. The summer camp I attended as a kid is shutting down after fifty years. They're asking for alumni to share a short memory for a printed keepsake book. What should I write?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. I reconnected with an old mentor I haven't spoken to in years — we're meeting for coffee next week. What's a good way to prepare for the conversation?

Keep your response practical and forward-looking. The small town where my grandparents lived is holding a reunion weekend for former residents — a lot of the families have since moved away. I'm considering going. How do I figure out if it's worth the trip?
